sentences.grammar_explanation: \"Hello world\" is a simple two-word exclamatory phrase. \"Hello\" is an interjection used to greet someone or attract attention — it has no grammatical inflection (no tense, number, or case). \"World\" is a singular common noun functioning as the object of address (a vocative), identifying who or what is being greeted. The phrase contains no verb, making it a nominal/verbless sentence. It is widely recognized as the classic first phrase used in programming tutorials to demonstrate basic output in a language.

tokens.morphology: Noun, singular, common noun – refers to the Earth or all people
